metadata.title: Incorporation of the Fasting Plasma FFA Concentration into QUICKI Improves Its Association with Insulin Sensitivity in Nonobese Individuals
metadata.journal: The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism
metadata.study_design: Euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp study in healthy nonobese individuals with or without first-degree relatives affected by type 2 diabetes mellitus
metadata.demographics: 57 young, healthy, nonobese subjects (38F/19M), age 19-45 years, white race, BMI <27 kg/m², sedentary lifestyle, no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking. 17 individuals with at least one parent with type 2 diabetes; 40 with no family history of diabetes

evidence.quote: GIANLUCA PERSEGHIN, ANDREA CAUMO, MARIANNA CALONI, GIULIO TESTOLIN, AND LIVIO LUZI
we performed a euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp [40mU/(m2·min)] in 57 young, healthy, nonobese individuals with (n = 17) or without (n = 40) first-degree relatives affected by type2diabetes

Fifty-seven healthy, young, nonobese subjects were recruited at the Istituto Scientifico H San Raffaele. The main criteria for their inclusion in the study were the following: 1) age (19–45 yr); 2) white race; 3) body mass index less than 27 kg/m2; 4) sedentary lifestyle; and 5) no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking.
we performed a euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp [40mU/(m2•min)] in 57 young, healthy, nonobese individuals
The main criteria for their inclusion in the study were the following: 1) age (19–45 yr)
body mass index less than 27 kg/m2
Subjects were studied by means of the euglycemic-hyperinsulinemic clamp to assess insulin sensitivity after a 10-hour overnight fast period

methods_participants.label: Participants with Family History of Type 2 Diabetes

evidence.quote: Seventeen individuals resulted to have at least one parent with type 2 diabetes
Subjects were instructed to consume an isocaloric diet and to abstain from exercise activity for 3 wk before the studies
Plasma glucose was measured with a glucose analyzer (Beckman Coulter, Inc., Fullerton, CA)
Plasma was then frozen and stored at •70C, and FFA determinations were performed as previously described

methods_participants.label: Dose-response study - low dose target plasma insulin

evidence.quote: The low-dose insulin clamp was designed to reach appropriate (150 pmol/liter) plasma insulin levels

narrative_results.statement: FFA levels during the low-dose insulin clamp were inversely associated with S I(clamp) calculated during both the low- and high-dose insulin clamp

evidence.quote: FFA levels during the low-dose insulin clamp (which is more appropriate to study the antilipolytic insulin action) were inversely associated with the S I(clamp) calculated during both the low- (r (cid:1) -0.57, P (cid:1) 0.033) and high-dose insulin clamp (r (cid:1) - 0.58, P (cid:1) 0.028)

narrative_results.statement: The revised QUICKI found a significant reduction in insulin sensitivity in the offspring group (10%), whereas HOMA-IR and QUICKI did not detect any significant difference

evidence.quote: Fasting plasma glucose and FFA concentrations were significantly increased in the offspring of type 2 diabetic parents with respect to the normal subjects

narrative_results.statement: Log(I b) and log(FFA b) were selected as the best set of predictors of insulin resistance by multiple stepwise regression analysis

evidence.quote: The multiple stepwise regression analysis selected log(I b) and log(FFA b) as the best set of predictors of IR

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass_1 describes insulin infusion rate (10 mU/m2·min) while pass_2 describes subject count (14 subjects); these are fundamentally different facts
Pass_1 describes target plasma glucose (5 mmol/liter) while pass_2 describes blood sampling frequency (every 15 minutes); completely different parameters
Pass_1 describes target plasma insulin (350 pmol/liter) while pass_2 describes fasting period (10 hours overnight); unrelated facts
Pass_1 describes blood sampling frequency (15 minutes) while pass_2 describes low-dose insulin infusion rate (10 mU/m2·min); different parameters
Pass 1 describes FFA storage temperature (-70°C) while Pass 2 describes FFA measurement timing (0-5 working days); these are different parameters

semantic_groups.rationale: Both describe the same participant count of 57, just with different label phrasing
Both describe insulin infusion rate of 40 mU/(m2·min) in euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp; pass_1 specifies 'High Dose' while pass_2 omits this qualifier
Pass_1 describes insulin infusion rate (10 mU/m2·min) while pass_2 describes subject count (14 subjects); these are fundamentally different facts
Both describe clamp duration of 150 minutes; negligible formatting difference
Pass_1 describes target plasma glucose (5 mmol/liter) while pass_2 describes blood sampling frequency (every 15 minutes); completely different parameters
Pass_1 describes target plasma insulin (350 pmol/liter) while pass_2 describes fasting period (10 hours overnight); unrelated facts
Pass_1 describes blood sampling frequency (15 minutes) while pass_2 describes low-dose insulin infusion rate (10 mU/m2·min); different parameters
Both describe age inclusion criteria of 19-45 years; negligible formatting difference
Both express BMI < 27 kg/m2 inclusion criteria with identical numerical threshold, only minor wording differences
Pass 1 contains race inclusion criteria (White) but pass 2 is empty; information only in one extraction
Pass 1 specifies 10-hour fasting period but pass 2 is empty; information only in one extraction
Both report identical count of 17 participants with family history of Type 2 Diabetes
Both report 40 participants without family history of diabetes; pass 1 omits 'Type 2' but meaning is equivalent
Pass 1 specifies dose-response study subgroup N=14 but pass 2 is empty; information only in one extraction
Pass 1 defines steady-state period as 120-150 minutes in euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp but pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 specifies isocaloric diet instruction but pass 2 is empty; information only in one extraction
Pass 1 contains pre-study exercise abstinence requirement (3 weeks), but Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains menstrual cycle timing specification (Days 3-8), but Pass 2 is empty
Both extractions describe identical plasma glucose measurement method using Beckman Coulter glucose analyzer
Both describe plasma insulin measurement via microparticle enzyme immunoassay; Pass 1 specifies IMx assay while Pass 2 uses generic technology description
Pass 1 contains FFA sample collection details (prechilled tubes with EDTA), but Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 describes FFA storage temperature (-70°C) while Pass 2 describes FFA measurement timing (0-5 working days); these are different parameters
Both extractions describe identical study design using euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp methodology
Identical text and meaning across both extractions
Same fact with minor phrasing differences: 'with respect to' vs 'compared to', and 'difference between groups' vs 'increase' as outcome labels
Same fact; pass_2 adds explicit comparison 'with respect to normal subjects' which is implicit in pass_1
Identical factual content with negligible difference in outcome label ('detection of difference' vs 'detection difference')
Identical text and meaning across both extractions
Same fact; pass_1 specifies 'healthy, nonobese individuals' while pass_2 omits this qualifier
Same fact with minor preposition difference: 'by multiple stepwise regression' vs 'in multiple stepwise regression'
Pass_1 contains a complete statement while pass_2 is empty; orphan entry from pass_1
Both extractions are identical, describing the same inverse association between SI(clamp), M value, GIR and fasting FFA with no wording differences.
